description: Minimal permissions for the dashboard. The frontend only calls custom #[tauri::command] handlers via invoke() (which itself needs no permission). We deliberately avoid `core:default` because it bundles broad permissions (window, webview, fs path, os info, resources, image, menu, tray) that the dashboard never uses — granting them widens the IPC attack surface for nothing. NO dialog:* permissions either: every dialog the app shows (close-confirm, show_confirm_dialog) is initiated from RUST via app.dialog(), which the capability system does not gate — the webview JS never imports the dialog plugin, so granting dialog:allow-message/confirm/ask would only let a future XSS foothold pop arbitrary native OS dialogs (system-prompt spoofing) for zero functionality.
